objective: Deliver a runnable loopback-only control plane with health/version/config endpoints plus a bounded WebSocket realtime transport for validated EventEnvelope v1 JSON and non-base64 binary media frames.
non_goals: Do not expose the service outside loopback, add browser authentication, TLS termination or a public network deployment.
Do not implement service supervision, model inference, speech, memory, avatar, game or livestream behavior.
Do not add an external HTTP or WebSocket dependency and do not run repeat/soak/full workflow verification.
contracts: The server binds only to a numeric loopback address and refuses non-loopback hosts before opening a socket.
GET /v1/health, /v1/version and /v1/config return bounded JSON responses and never expose secrets.
The /v1/realtime WebSocket accepts only RFC 6455 version 13 masked client frames and enforces explicit header, text, binary and connection limits.
Text event messages pass the existing EventEnvelope v1 runtime validator; durable envelopes are journaled before an acceptance response.
Binary media uses a fixed versioned header and a WebSocket binary frame; media bytes are never embedded as base64 JSON.
Malformed, oversized, unknown or unmasked input fails closed with a stable protocol error and a redacted local log record.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and the existing local hina-contracts and hina-core runtime packages.
oss_candidates: RFC 6455 protocol semantics as an interoperability reference; no upstream source code is copied.
acceptance_criteria: A single demo command starts the server on an ephemeral loopback port and reports healthy control endpoints.
A valid EventEnvelope v1 crosses WebSocket as JSON and receives an acceptance response; a duplicate durable envelope is acknowledged without another journal row.
A binary media payload round-trips as opcode 2 with its media identifier, sequence and bytes intact and with no base64 JSON field.
Invalid JSON/event input is rejected with a stable error code and produces a redacted JSONL error record.
Focused unit tests cover loopback enforcement, control endpoints, WebSocket text/binary frames, durable duplicate suppression and protocol rejection.
